dashboard.label: DMA Benchmark — tstats vs search
dashboard.description: Side-by-side comparison of |tstats (accelerated datamodel) vs |search (raw events) on the same dataset, plus per-datamodel acceleration health. Demonstrates the latency win from Data Model Acceleration.

search.query: | rest /servicesNS/-/-/data/models splunk_server=local | search acceleration=1 | eval datamodel = title | eval earliest_time = 'acceleration.earliest_time' | eval cron = 'acceleration.cron_schedule' | join type=outer datamodel [ | rest /servicesNS/-/-/admin/summarization splunk_server=local | eval datamodel = mvindex(split(summary_id, "_"), -1) | stats values(size) as bytes_summary values(access_count) as access_count values(is_inprogress) as build_in_progress values(complete) as completeness by datamodel ] | eval size_mb = round(bytes_summary/1024/1024, 2) | eval completeness_pct = round(completeness*100, 1) | table datamodel earliest_time cron size_mb completeness_pct build_in_progress access_count | sort datamodel
| tstats count from datamodel=Change where earliest=-24h | eval label = "events via tstats"
search index=main sourcetype=aws:cloudtrail earliest=-24h | stats count
| tstats count from datamodel=Change where earliest=-24h by Change.action, Change.object_category | sort -count | head 20
| tstats count from datamodel=Authentication where earliest=-24h by Authentication.action, Authentication.app | sort -count | head 20

body.p: This POC enables Data Model Acceleration (DMA) on the Change and Authentication CIM datamodels. Every 5 minutes Splunk pre-summarizes the last 7 days of matching events into tsidx files. |tstats reads those summary files directly, skipping the raw-event scan that |search performs.

ul.li: Latency. |tstats returns in tens of milliseconds even over millions of events. |search scales linearly with event volume.
Detections. Splunk ES correlation searches and most ESCU detections run as |tstats-style queries against CIM datamodels. Without acceleration the detection schedule falls behind real-time.
Cost. Accelerated datamodels reduce search-head CPU and search-peer disk I/O. The trade-off is index storage (tsidx summaries are typically ~10–20% of raw event size).
completeness_pct = 100 — summary build caught up to head of data. Searches return full-accuracy results.
completeness_pct < 100 — build still backfilling. |tstats summariesonly=t will under-count; summariesonly=f falls back to raw search for the unsummarized window.
build_in_progress = 1 — a summary-build search is running right now.
size_mb — current on-disk size of the tsidx summaries.
